description: Study 5/6 follow-up: content analysis of forced_visual verdict grounding. Classify each of the 47 non-empty viz_verdict self-reports (48 genuine forced_visual cells, Studies 5+6) by whether the verdict text cites NUMERIC scalar magnitudes (decimal/scientific values) and/or RELATIONAL curve-position language (above/below/sandwich/brackets-between/ordering). CPU-only keyword heuristic over existing transcripts; png_read_back deliberately NOT used as a 'did it look' signal because math-viz returns images inline.
motivation: a-0005/a-0006 asserted forced_visual verdicts ground in numeric metadata 'never a visual property read by eye', supported only by hand-picked 1985.B5 examples. Quantify it across all forced_visual cells and test whether the 'never visual' claim survives the inequality/ordering problem.
formal_results: 47/48 forced_visual cells had a non-empty verdict. Grounding: NUMERIC_only 19/47, RELATIONAL_only 13/47, BOTH 14/47, NEITHER 1/47 (any-numeric 33/47, any-relational 27/47). By problem: 1985.B5 11/11 numeric, 0 relational; 1991.A5 9 numeric / 8 relational; 1993.A1 12/12 numeric / 8 relational; 1996.B2 1 numeric / 11 relational. png_read_back True in 0/48.
results_description: Verdict grounding is problem-structure-dependent. On scalar-answer integral problems numeric grounding is near-universal (1985.B5 11/11, 1993.A1 12/12). On the double-inequality/ordering problem (1996.B2) verdicts are 11/12 relational ('lies above', 'sandwich', 'brackets between') and only 1/12 numeric.
conclusions: Qualifies a-0005: the verdict text DOES use visual/relational properties - but only when the claim's truth is an ordering rather than a number; on scalar problems it is numeric. Does NOT overturn modality equivalence: forced_textual caught 1996.B2 equally (Study 6), so the relational property is one numeric sampling captures just as well. The 'never a visual property' phrasing should be narrowed to scalar-answer problems.
